name: engram
description: Token-first, cross-project long-term memory for Claude Code. Captures sessions off the interactive path, distils atomic facts, embeds them compactly, and injects relevant memory just-in-time via hooks. Local-first — no API key, no network.

embedding.title: Embedding backend
embedding.description: hash = dependency-free lexical stub (zero installs); fastembed = real semantic model. On first use, fastembed self-provisions a private venv under the plugin data dir — no manual pip needed.

embedding_model.title: Embedding model (fastembed)
embedding_model.description: fastembed model id. Blank uses BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 (best measured recall). BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 is lighter/faster.

embedding_truncate_dim.title: Embedding truncation (Matryoshka)
embedding_truncate_dim.description: Keep only the first N vector dimensions and re-normalise (0 = off). Only sensible for Matryoshka-trained models (e.g. nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5), where 256 shrinks bytes/fact ~3x with modest recall loss. Changing this invalidates stored vectors — re-capture/re-index afterwards.

python.title: Pinned interpreter (advanced)
python.description: Absolute path to a Python with fastembed, used for all hooks. Leave blank to use the auto-provisioned managed venv.

distiller.title: Distiller
distiller.description: claude = headless claude -p (defaults to Haiku — best-quality outcome facts, costs a little); ollama = local OpenAI-compatible server (zero tokens, offline); heuristic = dependency-free line extraction (no LLM). claude/ollama fall back to heuristic on any failure, so capture never breaks.

distiller_model.title: Distiller model
distiller_model.description: For claude: model alias/id (blank = haiku). For ollama: model name (blank = qwen2.5:3b).

distiller_base_url.title: Distiller base URL (ollama/http)
distiller_base_url.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint for the ollama/http distiller. Default is a local Ollama server.

antipatterns.title: Anti-pattern catalogue
antipatterns.description: Mine sessions for mistakes the assistant admitted and store them as durable 'antipattern' memories (a strict rule + do/don't), surfaced later to prevent repeats. Gated by a cheap admission-marker scan and throttled like the session summary; runs in the detached capture worker. No-op unless an LLM distiller is configured.

ttl_days.title: Memory TTL (days)
ttl_days.description: Archive facts unseen for this many days on capture. 0 disables hard expiry (recency decay still de-ranks old facts).

ttl_keep_frequency.title: TTL keep-frequency
ttl_keep_frequency.description: Facts reinforced at least this many times are never expired by the TTL sweep.

top_k.title: Recall results per prompt
top_k.description: Max facts injected just-in-time on each prompt — the small 'focus of attention' (Cowan ~4).

activated_k.title: On-demand recall breadth
activated_k.description: How many facts the on-demand `recall` MCP tool searches — the broader 'activated long-term memory' beyond the injected focus. Blank/0 uses top_k, so the two-level split is off until raised. Does not affect per-prompt injected tokens.

min_sim.title: Similarity threshold
min_sim.description: Minimum cosine similarity for a fact to be injected. Higher = stricter (fewer, more relevant).

core_size.title: Session core size
core_size.description: Facts injected once at session start (cache-friendly). Set 0 to disable.

core_scaffold.title: Structured session core
core_scaffold.description: Render the session core as a titled scaffold (facts grouped under their card title) instead of a flat list — a long-term-working-memory retrieval structure. Same character budget; off by default (flat).

max_chars.title: Max injected characters
max_chars.description: Hard cap on characters injected per hook — the token budget guard.

index_top_k.title: Index hits per prompt
index_top_k.description: Passive code/docs index hits injected just-in-time on each prompt (0 = off). FTS-prefiltered then cosine-reranked, so it stays cheap regardless of index size.

index_min_sim.title: Index similarity threshold
index_min_sim.description: Minimum cosine similarity for an indexed chunk to be injected passively. Higher = stricter.

index_max_chars.title: Max injected index characters
index_max_chars.description: Hard cap on characters for the passive index block per prompt.

cross_project.title: Cross-project fallback
cross_project.description: If in-project recall is weak, fall back to other projects' memory (slightly penalised).

identity.title: Project identity
identity.description: workspace = key memory on the folder you opened (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, else cwd), so a monorepo subfolder stays its own project; marker = walk up to the nearest project marker (.git etc.). A .engram-root file overrides both.

half_life_days.title: Recency half-life (days)
half_life_days.description: A memory's recency weight halves every this-many days unless reinforced. Lower = forgets faster.

supersede_threshold.title: Supersession similarity
supersede_threshold.description: A new fact this similar (cosine) to an older one archives the older (newest wins). 1.0 disables. Keep high to avoid retiring distinct facts.

recall_min_confidence.title: Recall confidence gate
recall_min_confidence.description: The `recall` MCP tool returns verdict=ok only when calibrated confidence reaches this; below it the verdict is low_confidence (treat facts as hints, widen the search). Higher = stricter.

recall_max_chars.title: Recall response budget (chars)
recall_max_chars.description: Character budget for facts returned by the on-demand `recall` MCP tool — highest-scoring facts are packed until it is spent.

viewer_autostart.title: Auto-start memory viewer
viewer_autostart.description: Start the localhost memory viewer as a detached background process on session start. It stays up across sessions (only one instance runs) and streams live updates. Disable to start it manually with /memory-viewer.

viewer_port.title: Memory viewer port
viewer_port.description: Localhost port for the always-on memory viewer.

promote_after_freq.title: STM→LTM promote-after frequency
promote_after_freq.description: Reinforcement count at which a fresh short-term fact is promoted to the long-term store (rehearsal).

stm_capacity.title: Short-term capacity
stm_capacity.description: Max active short-term facts before the weakest are displaced (archived). Ships as a generous, non-destructive backstop against runaway STM growth — displacement is a reversible status flip. 0 = unbounded (displacement off).

stm_recall_weight.title: STM recall weight
stm_recall_weight.description: Recall weight applied to short-term facts. 1.0 = tier-agnostic (default); below 1.0 down-ranks STM at recall.

spread_weight.title: Associative spreading weight
spread_weight.description: Spreading activation over the fact association graph (ACT-R). 0 = off (default): no edges recorded at capture, no spread at recall — store and hot path untouched. Above 0 records co-occurrence/shared-entity edges and boosts candidates co-activated with other retrieved facts. Retrieval-affecting — tune with `engram eval` before enabling.

sensory_enabled.title: Sensory register
sensory_enabled.description: Atkinson-Shiffrin sensory register: a single modality-tagged intake stage all perception enters (page a11y snapshots + conversation), holding raw text briefly and decaying unless attended. Foundation only — intake hooks and promotion land in later phases; the table and sweep are inert until then.

attention_window_seconds.title: Attention window (seconds)
attention_window_seconds.description: Window within which a re-perception of the same page counts as attention (the A-S selective read-out that promotes a perception to the durable store). Attention, not rehearsal.

sensory_capacity.title: Sensory capacity
sensory_capacity.description: Max live (undecayed) perceptions per project before the oldest unattended ones decay from the register (displacement). Attended perceptions are protected until promoted. 0 = unbounded.

sensory_ttl_seconds.title: Sensory TTL (seconds)
sensory_ttl_seconds.description: How long an unattended perception lives in the register before it decays ('lost from SR'); decayed tombstones are hard-purged past this age so the table stays bounded. 0 = no TTL decay.

integrate_threshold.title: Integrate threshold (merge)
integrate_threshold.description: Consolidation collapses near-duplicate short-term facts whose cosine similarity is at least this, keeping one survivor (reversible status='merged'). Ships on at 0.92 — a low-risk near-identical mop-up sitting above supersede_threshold (0.85), which handles the bulk of dedup on the write path. Below supersede_threshold clusters at the gist level; 0 = disabled.

refine_keep_max.title: Refine keep-max (prune)
refine_keep_max.description: Consolidation keeps only the top-N active facts by retention score and prunes the rest (reversible status='pruned'). Ships on at 20000 as a generous, idempotent ceiling — only fires on runaway growth, unlike refine_prune_percentile which forgets every pass. 0 = disabled.

refine_prune_percentile.title: Refine prune percentile
refine_prune_percentile.description: Consolidation prunes the lowest-retention active facts each pass. A value in (0,1) is a self-limiting percentile of the current active set (0.1 = drop the weakest 10%); a value >=1 is an absolute retention-score floor. 0 = disabled. Retrieval-affecting — enable deliberately.

purge_horizon_days.title: Purge horizon (days)
purge_horizon_days.description: Hard-delete (and VACUUM) facts that have been archived longer than this many days. 0 = disabled (archives are kept, reversible).

bus.title: Work-queue backend
bus.description: Durable queue for detached capture/recovery. inproc = zero-dependency SQLite queue (default); nats = NATS JetStream (opt-in). Fails open to inproc if NATS is unavailable.

bus_max_deliver.title: Queue max deliveries
bus_max_deliver.description: Delivery attempts before a work item is dead-lettered.

bus_backoff.title: Queue retry backoff
bus_backoff.description: Retry backoff schedule in seconds, comma-separated (e.g. 5,30,120,600).

lease_ttl.title: Queue lease TTL (seconds)
lease_ttl.description: Seconds a claimed work item is leased before it can be reclaimed (crash recovery).

bus_dead_after.title: Queue dead-letter age (seconds)
bus_dead_after.description: A pending work item unprocessed for longer than this is dead-lettered (kept for inspection), so an item no active backend ever pulls can't accumulate forever. Default 7 days; 0 disables.

nats_url.title: NATS URL
nats_url.description: NATS server URL when bus=nats. Use a dedicated port so engram doesn't share another project's server.

nats_stream.title: NATS stream name
nats_stream.description: JetStream stream name for the work queue.

nats_provision.title: NATS auto-provision
nats_provision.description: How to start NATS when bus=nats and none is reachable: binary = download + run nats-server (no Docker needed); docker = run the official image; off = bring your own.

nats_version.title: NATS server version
nats_version.description: Pinned nats-server version for the binary provisioner (checksum-verified download).
